short_text: Mark Adair to Brandon Glover, Caught by Curtis Campher!! Third wicket for Adair. He now has the most economical figures
full_text: Mark Adair to Brandon Glover, Caught by Curtis Campher!! Third wicket for Adair. He now has the most economical figures for Ireland in T20 World Cups. Another slower ball that does the trick, Glover goes for the big swing and skies it straight up, Campher settles near mid-off and hangs on comfortably.

short_text: Mark Adair to van Beek, van Beek Run Out!! 1 run completed. van Beek wanted to keep strike and hence pushed for the non-
full_text: Mark Adair to van Beek, van Beek Run Out!! 1 run completed. van Beek wanted to keep strike and hence pushed for the non-existent second and even a full length dive is not enough to save him. A full delivery that he flicks to deep square leg, charges back for the second but Little's throw is right next to the stumps and Neil Rock makes no mistake.

short_text: Mark Adair to Seelaar, Caught by Balbirnie!! What a spell it has been from Adair. Just mind-blowing figures. The leg-cut
full_text: Mark Adair to Seelaar, Caught by Balbirnie!! What a spell it has been from Adair. Just mind-blowing figures. The leg-cutter is dragged short and Seelaar has to generate all the power, he slaps it towards long-on but does not have the elevation to clear Balbirnie who takes it well inside the rope.

short_text: Mark Adair to van Beek, 1 run, now goes full and straight, van Beek flicks to deep backward square
full_text: Mark Adair to van Beek, 1 run, now goes full and straight, van Beek flicks to deep backward square

short_text: Mark Adair to van Beek, no run, change of pace and he totally deceives van Beek, he's through his shot a day early and t
full_text: Mark Adair to van Beek, no run, change of pace and he totally deceives van Beek, he's through his shot a day early and the ball gently lobs over the top-edge, goes on the bounce to Neil Rock

short_text: Mark Adair to van Beek, 2 runs, very full delivery and van Beek jams it straight down the ground, calls for two and Seel
full_text: Mark Adair to van Beek, 2 runs, very full delivery and van Beek jams it straight down the ground, calls for two and Seelaar scampers back to make his crease

short_text: Joshua Little to van Beek, 1 run, full toss and van Beek swings it away to deep midwicket, just a single
full_text: Joshua Little to van Beek, 1 run, full toss and van Beek swings it away to deep midwicket, just a single

short_text: Joshua Little to Seelaar, leg byes, 1 run, and he misses again.. He walks across his stumps to play the scoop, but it is
full_text: Joshua Little to Seelaar, leg byes, 1 run, and he misses again.. He walks across his stumps to play the scoop, but it is another fine yorker and he is struck on the pad, van Beek calls for the single and Seelaar had given him as Little has a shy at the non-striker's end

short_text: Joshua Little to Seelaar, no run, another one right in the blockhole, Seelaar jams it out to Little and he fails to hit
full_text: Joshua Little to Seelaar, no run, another one right in the blockhole, Seelaar jams it out to Little and he fails to hit the stumps at the bowler's end. , even his full length dive wouldn't have saved him

short_text: Joshua Little to Seelaar, no run, and again.. Little nails the yorker outside off and Seelaar cannot squeeze it out, van
full_text: Joshua Little to Seelaar, no run, and again.. Little nails the yorker outside off and Seelaar cannot squeeze it out, van Beek asks umpire Erasmus why it was not given a wide and he explains that the batter had moved

short_text: Joshua Little to van Beek, 1 run, once more walks across premeditatedly and drives down to long-off
full_text: Joshua Little to van Beek, 1 run, once more walks across premeditatedly and drives down to long-off

full_text: Joshua Little to van Beek, no run, wide yorker outside off and van Beek misses out on the drive, umpire Marais Erasmus does not call it a wide as the batter had moved across

full_text: Simi Singh to van Beek, 1 run, shortens the length and van Beek knocks it to long-on, retains strike

full_text: Simi Singh to van Beek, 2 runs, comes down the track and is almost deceived by the straighter one, the outside edge runs away fine and Stirling manages to haul it in before the rope, two more

short_text: Mark Adair to Max ODowd, Caught by Harry Tector!! ODowd's vigil comes to an end. Smart bowling from Adair. He's deserved
full_text: Mark Adair to Max ODowd, Caught by Harry Tector!! ODowd's vigil comes to an end. Smart bowling from Adair. He's deserved this wicket. Rolled his fingers over the ball and deceived ODowd by the lack of pace, the Dutch opener does not get the power behind the heave and Tector settles right on the boundary edge near long-on to take the catch.

full_text: Curtis Campher to Max ODowd, , poor delivery. Went for the cross-seam yorker and slips it down leg, ODowd gets a fine tickle and the ball races away to the fence

full_text: Simi Singh to Seelaar, 1 run, spotted Seelaar charge down and darted it down leg, the Dutch skipper adjusted quickly and managed to nurdle it backward of square. He was gone had he missed that

short_text: Curtis Campher to van der Merwe, Bowled!! What is happening? Absolute madness in Abu Dhabi as Campher gets four in four
full_text: Curtis Campher to van der Merwe, Bowled!! What is happening? Absolute madness in Abu Dhabi as Campher gets four in four becoming the third bowler in T20 history to do so. Rashid Khan, Lasith Malinga, and Curtis Campher. Wow. Full and wide, van der Merwe throws his hands at it and drags it back on.

short_text: Curtis Campher to Scott Edwards, Lbw!! Oh that must be close! The umpire says no but Ireland take the review. Pitched ri
full_text: Curtis Campher to Scott Edwards, Lbw!! Oh that must be close! The umpire says no but Ireland take the review. Pitched right up on middle, Edwards plays across the line and misses. Gets hit very low on the front pad, and there's no bat involved. It's three reds and it's a ! Wow does this game change quickly or what. Had started poorly in his spell, got a lucky breakthrough down leg and now has the first hat-trick in the T20 World Cup 2021. Netherlands in disarray.

short_text: Curtis Campher to ten Doeschate, Lbw!! Campher has two in two! And it's the wicket of ten Doeschate who's been given out
full_text: Curtis Campher to ten Doeschate, Lbw!! Campher has two in two! And it's the wicket of ten Doeschate who's been given out LBW first ball. He doesn't bother to review. Full and straight, looks to play across the line but misses. Was it going down leg? Need to have another look. Ireland are jubilant.

short_text: Curtis Campher to Ackermann, Caught by Neil Rock!! Short down leg, Ackermann looks to pull but it goes through to the ke
full_text: Curtis Campher to Ackermann, Caught by Neil Rock!! Short down leg, Ackermann looks to pull but it goes through to the keeper. Campher appeals enthusiastically. It's been signalled wide, and Ireland have taken the review. Oh there's glove! UltraEdge clearly picks it up. So the decision will be overturned. A bonus for Campher who hasn't started well with the ball and this wasn't a great delivery either.

full_text: Joshua Little to Max ODowd, leg byes, 1 run, an appeal as ODowd is smashed low on the front pad. Not out. Full ball slanted in from round the wicket, ODowd plants his front leg too far across and then his bat can't come through. May have missed leg stump with the angle

short_text: Stirling to Ben Cooper, Ben Cooper Run Out!! That's a horrendous call for a single. Cooper is barely even halfway down t
full_text: Stirling to Ben Cooper, Ben Cooper Run Out!! That's a horrendous call for a single. Cooper is barely even halfway down the pitch when the bails are taken off at the non-striker's end. Sliding into leg, he clips it to square leg. ODowd charges through and Cooper reluctantly obliges. Stirling has a simple job to do - just gather the throw and take the bails off. But he almost makes a hash of it. He dislodged one of the bails with his arm before he had collected the throw. The other one stayed on though, and the third umpire confirms that before signalling out. Seeing the replay now, and it's clear that Cooper hesitated initially. Maybe he would've made it had he just taken off. But it would've been tight anyway.

full_text: Stirling to Max ODowd, 1 run, another outswinger, ODowd clips it to long-on

full_text: Seelaar to Gareth Delany, Bowled!! Knocked him over! Walks down the wicket does Gareth Delany and Seelaar goes fuller. The ball sneaks through as it was too full and strikes timber. End of a pivotal innings by Gareth Delany as Ireland are not far away from an emphatic win.

full_text: van der Merwe to Gareth Delany, , a huge grunt by van der Merwe as he seems frustrated. Had there been a slip, this could have been a straight forward chance. Ireland cruising along in the run chase as it's time for

full_text: Fred Klaassen to Balbirnie, , overcompensates with his length and pays the price. This short ball sits up nicely for Balbirnie and he pulls in front of square. There was a fielder in the deep but he had placed it perfectly

full_text: Fred Klaassen to Balbirnie, , that's not great execution. With no third man Klaassen offers width outside off, Balbirnie slices the drive and gets it behind square, splitting the slip fielder and short third man

short_text: Fred Klaassen to Stirling, , another wide. This is down leg and Stirling once again is content to let it pass..
full_text: Fred Klaassen to Stirling, , another wide. This is down leg and Stirling once again is content to let it pass..

short_text: Fred Klaassen to Stirling, no run, closer to the off-stump and Stirling has to play this time, he has a tentative poke a
full_text: Fred Klaassen to Stirling, no run, closer to the off-stump and Stirling has to play this time, he has a tentative poke and is beaten on the outside edge. The ball does not carry to Edwards this time

short_text: Fred Klaassen to Stirling, , tight call from Erasmus. On a length and outside off, Stirling does not bother and it was c
full_text: Fred Klaassen to Stirling, , tight call from Erasmus. On a length and outside off, Stirling does not bother and it was close to the wide marker

short_text: Fred Klaassen to Stirling, byes, , more bonus runs for Ireland. Bouncer angled across and it keeps swirling in the air,
full_text: Fred Klaassen to Stirling, byes, , more bonus runs for Ireland. Bouncer angled across and it keeps swirling in the air, Stirling sways out of the line and Edwards is not able to glove it because of all the movement

short_text: Fred Klaassen to Stirling, no run, shout for LBW. Erasmus is not interested and Netherlands also don't review. Swinging
full_text: Fred Klaassen to Stirling, no run, shout for LBW. Erasmus is not interested and Netherlands also don't review. Swinging into the right hander and Stirling falls over as he misses the flick, the ball had pitched outside leg and was swinging down

short_text: Fred Klaassen to Stirling, , sprays it way down leg, Stirling is happy to leave and collect the extra
full_text: Fred Klaassen to Stirling, , sprays it way down leg, Stirling is happy to leave and collect the extra

short_text: Fred Klaassen to Stirling, no run, fuller and hint of inward movement, Stirling prods forward and defends solidly
full_text: Fred Klaassen to Stirling, no run, fuller and hint of inward movement, Stirling prods forward and defends solidly
